title: pi-jit-agents consumer migration arc
description: Migrate pi-workflows and pi-behavior-monitors to import agent infrastructure from @davidorex/pi-jit-agents. Resolve the provider-pin, thinking-seam, and cross-consumer duplication at the correct boundary (pi-jit-agents executeAgent). Close the cluster of open issues that are blocked on this migration: 043 (duplicate agent infrastructure), 045 (framework-level anti-injection), 048 (agent discovery contradiction), 049 (step-loop cwd bug), 050 (monitor classify wiring).
motivation: The platonic first enactment of pi-jit-agents landed in commit 239f718 but consumers still carry their own copies of agent infrastructure. This duplication is load-bearing: the model-pin that breaks monitors under OpenRouter-only auth lives inside a consumer copy of parseModelSpec, not at the framework boundary. Fixing it in the consumer creates throwaway work since the code is about to move. Fixing it at the correct boundary (executeAgent in pi-jit-agents) resolves the pin and the duplication in one arc. The arc is also the operational-pattern test case for the Muni five-layer model enacted against PLAN-001 — how it plays out here informs what pi-project must eventually generate natively per the framework gaps in FGAP-001 through FGAP-006.
acceptance_criteria: DEC-0001, DEC-0002, DEC-0003 all advance to decided state via user-authored transitions
parseModelSpec lives in pi-jit-agents at the execute boundary; pi-behavior-monitors' copy is removed
executeAgent enforces the thinking-seam policy per DEC-0002 with explicit warning
pi-behavior-monitors' classifyViaAgent delegates to pi-jit-agents executeAgent, carrying no duplicate agent-spec or template code
pi-workflows' agent-step dispatch delegates to pi-jit-agents executeAgent, removing step-shared.ts and local agent-spec/template helpers
All five bundled classifier YAMLs in pi-behavior-monitors/agents/ align with the decided DEC-0001 and DEC-0002 policies
Issues 043, 045, 048, 049, 050 close or migrate to scope-owned registries
Empirical verification: hedge monitor runs successfully against an OpenRouter-routed Claude model
Empirical verification: hedge monitor runs successfully against at least one Kimi model (via openrouter or via kimi-coding direct)
All tests in npm test remain at 0 failures
